content:
# Welcome to ArunCode

ArunCode is a suite of tools built by **Arun Chandrasekaran**.

## What's Included

- **ArunCode_Term** — A terminal environment for managing your system
- **ArunCode IDE** — A full code editor with file saving and folder creation
- **ArunCode_Emails** — Send and receive messages between users
- **Python Teaching Club** — Learn Python with lessons and quizzes
- **ArunCode Docs** — You are here!

## Roles

| Role | Permissions |
|---|---|
| admin | Full access — edit docs, manage users |
| security | View users, access logs, delete regular users |
| user | Access all apps, read docs |
title: Welcome to ArunCode

content:
# ArunCode_Term Guide

ArunCode_Term is the terminal dashboard application.

## Logging In

1. Go to `/terminal`
2. Enter your email and username
3. Enter your password
4. **Admin accounts** require a 6-digit PIN as a second factor

## Dashboard Commands

```
help     — show all commands
status   — system status
version  — current version
whoami   — logged in user
date     — current date/time
ide      — open ArunCode IDE
logout   — end your session
clear    — clear the console
```
title: ArunCode_Term Guide

content:
# ArunCode IDE

A browser-based code editor with file saving and folder support.

## Features

- Syntax highlighting for Python, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, JSON
- Multiple tabs
- File tree sidebar
- Save to server — files persist in the `workspace/` folder
- Create folders
- Run code — execute JavaScript directly, simulate Python output
- Line numbers

## Saving Files

Files are saved server-side in the `workspace/` directory next to `app.py`.

## Running Code

- **JavaScript** — executes live in the browser
- **Python** — simulates `print()` output
title: IDE User Guide

content:
# Python Teaching Club

Learn Python through structured lessons with quizzes.

## Lessons

| # | Title | Level |
|---|---|---|
| 01 | Hello, Python! | Beginner |
| 02 | Variables & Data Types | Beginner |
| 03 | If Statements | Beginner |
| 04 | Loops | Intermediate |
| 05 | Functions | Intermediate |

## How to Use

1. Go to `/ptc`
2. Click any lesson card to open it
3. Read through the lesson and code examples
4. Answer the quiz question
5. Click **MARK AS DONE** to track your progress
title: Python Teaching Club

content:
# ArunCode Emails

An internal messaging system between ArunCode users.

## Sending an Email

1. Go to `/emails`
2. Click **+ COMPOSE**
3. In the **To** field, enter a username (e.g. `ArunC`) or their email address
4. Fill in Subject and body
5. Click **SEND**

## Folders

| Folder | Contents |
|---|---|
| Inbox | Emails sent to you |
| Sent | Emails you sent |
| Starred | Emails you starred |

> Emails only work between ArunCode users, not external Gmail.
title: Email System Guide
